name: Budget Planners
description_short: Budget Planners offers debt consolidation and credit counseling services in Detroit, Michigan, helping consumers merge multiple debts into manageable payment plans without loans.
description_long: Budget Planners operates a debt consolidation service targeting Detroit and Michigan residents struggling with multiple debts and high interest rates. The company markets itself as a solution for consumers juggling numerous credit card payments, particularly those paying high interest rates (19%+) across multiple cards. Their primary service model involves connecting consumers with certified debt settlement specialists who provide free consultations and custom consolidation solutions. The company emphasizes that their programs do not require taking out consolidation loans, instead focusing on restructuring existing debt through negotiation and payment planning. Budget Planners positions itself as an educational resource, claiming to combine debt consolidation programs with financial education to improve overall financial literacy and soundness. They operate through a lead generation model, collecting consumer information (debt amount, payment status, state, contact preferences) and routing qualified prospects to partner counselors. The website stresses free consultations and rapid relief, using language like 'Change Your Financial Future NOW' and 'getting you relief fast.' However, the content quality is notably poor, with repetitive messaging, grammatical errors, and unclear descriptions of actual service mechanisms. The company's business model appears to be lead aggregation and referral rather than direct debt negotiation, as evidenced by the footer disclosing submissions go to 'CuraDebt Systems, LLC' for counseling delivery. This raises questions about whether Budget Planners itself employs counselors or simply captures and sells leads to third parties.
pros: Offers free consultations and assessments with certified credit counselors
Provides custom consolidation solutions tailored to individual financial profiles
Claims to help consolidate multiple high-interest debts without requiring new loans
Includes financial education and budgeting guidance with consolidation programs
Serves Michigan consumers with localized landing pages (detroit.budgetplanners.net)
Works with consumers at various debt levels ($5K-$100K+) and payment statuses
Emphasizes fast relief and quick action through streamlined contact process
cons: Website content is poorly written with repetitive messaging and grammatical errors ('This's a simple way', excessive redundancy)
Business model unclear—appears to be a lead generator that sells referrals to CuraDebt Systems LLC rather than directly providing services
No transparent disclosure of partner company, fee structures, or how debt settlement negotiations actually work
No mention of debt settlement program risks, potential credit score impacts, or tax implications of forgiven debt
Vague about actual service delivery—unclear whether they negotiate with creditors or simply provide counseling
best_for: Michigan residents with $10K-$50K in unsecured credit card debt across multiple cards
Consumers paying 15%+ interest rates seeking to consolidate into single manageable payments
Individuals overwhelmed by multiple monthly payments who want free initial guidance before committing
diagnosis: Budget Planners is best for Michigan residents with multiple high-interest credit card debts seeking free initial guidance on consolidation options. The main caveat is that this appears to be a lead generation service rather than a direct service provider—consumers should clarify whether they're speaking with Budget Planners staff or referral partners, understand actual fee structures, and confirm the legitimacy of CuraDebt Systems LLC before proceeding.
services: Free debt consolidation consultations with certified counselors
Custom consolidation loan solutions tailored to financial profile
Debt settlement and negotiation programs (via partner CuraDebt Systems)
Monthly payment consolidation into single affordable payments
Financial education and money management training
Multi-state debt relief programs (operates across all 50 states)
Debt relief for consumers 1+ months past due through over a year delinquent
